source: Card issuer websites, NerdWallet, The Points Guy, Bankrate
dataAgeNote: Annual fees, APRs, and sign-up bonuses change frequently. RICHY SHOULD WEB SEARCH to verify current offers before recommending a specific card. Use this data for category rewards rates and general structure, which change less often.
disclaimer: Agent Richy is not a licensed financial advisor. Credit card recommendations are based on publicly available reward structures and the user's stated spending patterns. Always read the full terms before applying.

cards.name: Amex Blue Cash Preferred

apr.introOffer: 0% for 12 months on purchases and balance transfers

groceries.note: Best grocery card. 6% back = $360/year on $500/month grocery spend.

transit.cap: Taxis, rideshare, parking, tolls, trains, buses

cards.bestFor: Heavy grocery shoppers spending $300+/month on groceries
cards.breakEvenSpend: The $95 fee is covered if you spend $159/month on groceries (at 6% vs 1.5% on a flat-rate card)
cards.notIdealFor: Light grocery shoppers, people who shop at Walmart/Target (coded as superstore, not grocery)

cards.name: Chase Freedom Unlimited
cards.bestFor: Everyday spending card. 1.5% on everything with no annual fee. Great starter card.
cards.notIdealFor: People who want maximum returns in specific categories

cards.name: Citi Double Cash

everything.note: 1% when you buy + 1% when you pay. Effectively 2% flat on everything.

cards.bestFor: Simple, no-category-tracking 2% back on everything. Best flat-rate card.
cards.notIdealFor: People who want to maximize specific categories

cards.name: Chase Sapphire Preferred
cards.bestFor: Travelers and diners. Points worth 25% more through Chase Travel portal.
cards.breakEvenSpend: Spend ~$320/month on dining/travel to offset $95 fee vs a 1.5% card
cards.notIdealFor: People who don't travel or rarely eat out

cards.name: Capital One SavorOne
cards.bestFor: No annual fee with 3% on dining, entertainment, and groceries. Great all-arounder.
cards.notIdealFor: People wanting maximum groceries (Amex Blue Cash Preferred is better at 6%)

cards.name: Discover it Cash Back

signUpBonus.amount: Cashback Match — Discover matches ALL cash back earned in first year

rotating_quarterly.cap: $1,500/quarter in rotating categories (gas, groceries, Amazon, restaurants, etc.)

cards.bestFor: First year is incredible — effectively 10% back on quarterly categories and 2% on everything else due to the match. Great first card.
cards.notIdealFor: People who forget to activate quarterly categories

cards.name: Wells Fargo Active Cash
cards.bestFor: Another excellent flat 2% card. Competes directly with Citi Double Cash.

cards.name: Amazon Prime Rewards Visa
cards.bestFor: Amazon Prime members who spend $200+/month on Amazon. 5% back = $120+/year.
cards.breakEvenSpend: No card fee but requires Prime ($139/year). Need to spend ~$232/month on Amazon for 5% rewards to cover Prime cost vs a 2% card.
cards.notIdealFor: Non-Prime members or light Amazon shoppers

cards.name: Costco Anywhere Visa

gas.note: Best gas card at 4%. Costco gas is already cheapest.

cards.bestFor: Costco members who drive a lot. 4% on gas is best available. Combined with cheap Costco gas = maximum fuel savings.
cards.breakEvenSpend: No card fee but requires Costco membership ($65-130/year)

cards.name: US Bank Cash+

chosen_category_1.note: YOU CHOOSE two 5% categories: utilities, internet/streaming, cell phone, TV/internet, gym, fast food, furniture stores, department stores, electronics, select clothing

chosen_category_3.cap: Choose one 2% category: gas, groceries, restaurants

cards.bestFor: The only card that gives 5% on UTILITIES and CELL PHONE BILLS. If your electric + internet + phone = $300/month, that's $180/year back. Nobody else offers this.
cards.notIdealFor: People who don't want to manage category selections

twoCardCombo.name: The Two-Card Strategy
twoCardCombo.description: Use a high-category card for your top spending areas + a flat 2% card for everything else.

examples.name: Bills + Everything
examples.idealFor: Anyone with $300+/month in utility and phone bills

debtPayoff.name: Balance Transfer Strategy
debtPayoff.description: Transfer high-interest credit card debt to a 0% intro APR card to stop bleeding interest.
debtPayoff.warning: Balance transfer fees are typically 3-5%. Calculate whether the interest savings exceed the fee.

calculationFormula: For each spending category: monthlySpend × (newCardRate - currentCardRate) / 100 × 12 = annual reward difference. Subtract annual fee difference. Positive = switch is worth it.
